title: Accessible Names, Images, Language, Headings, Landmarks, and Human Review · Contract Workshop
summary: A public-interest site-quality team incrementally builds accessible page-quality audit with human-review handoff from original fixed response and HTML fixtures while retaining earlier authorization, HTTP, URL, provenance, politeness, accessibility, testing, security, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of accessible page-quality audit with human-review handoff while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit th…
objectives: Check declared page and part language, document title presence and purpose, and conflicts with observed content while retaining human-review limits.
Extract headings, labels, sections, and landmarks in document context and flag missing or misleading structure without enforcing a simplistic numeric ladder.
Classify informative, decorative, functional, complex, grouped, linked, and text images and assess alternative evidence in context.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Page Language Title
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop site-quality team handles case 3421 during a privacy-minimized internal crawl. Build deterministic Python evidence to audit language, title, structure, images, names, and relationships with evidence and mandatory human review; the prior valid checkpoint and report must survive failure; then introduce a block, private field, dangerous destination, or spreadsheet formula and stop or reject it. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Automated Accessibility Li…
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: scraper-automated-accessibility-limit-smerit" that uses case 6411 to audit language, title, structure, images, names, and relationships with evidence and mandatory human review. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, contact sites, import or claim native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, or Playwright behavior, read or write host files, use credentials, launch browsers, schedule host jobs, or claim live accessibility, privacy, legal, load, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, the recorded stable package versions, fixed and malformed fixtures, fake transports, controlled local servers, authorized disposable target sites, representative browser profiles, privacy, security, accessibility and legal review, load, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Authorized crawling and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Automated Accessibility Li….

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-automated-accessibility-limit-smerit
# Competency: scraper-automated-accessibility-limit.
# Case 6411: a clean package and recovery drill.
# Operating constraint: no block, CAPTCHA, identity, or fingerprint evasion is permitted.
# Changed case: inject one retry, cancellation, queue, checkpoint, or output failure and recover cleanly.
def evidence_scraper_automated_accessibility_limit_smerit(pages: int = 24, automated_findings: int = 38, manual_checks: int = 42, conformance_claims: int = 0, findings_with_evidence: int = 38) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_smerit = "smerit-tirems-6411"
    assert evidence_variant_smerit
    evidence_axes_smerit = (
        "authorizationsmerit",
        "url6411",
        "responsetirems",
        "treesmerit6411",
        "frontier6411smerit",
        "reporttirems6411",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_smerit)) == 6
    if pages < 1 or automated_findings < 0:
        return False, pages, "accessibility-count"
    if findings_with_evidence != automated_findings:
        return False, findings_with_evidence, "finding-evidence"
    if manual_checks < pages:
        return False, manual_checks, "manual-review-gap"
    if conformance_claims > 0:
        return False, conformance_claims, "conformance-overclaim"
    assert findings_with_evidence == automated_findings and conformance_claims == 0
    return True, automated_findings, "bounded-accessibility-audit"
